Typo – Here Again is the issue of the abbreviation "a." in italics, which indicates "adjective". Andhrabharati has stated that AP90 should always be consulted as a background check to AP57. In this very case, AP90 confirms: "Adjective" ! Therefore, in the Window Display, we have the additional correction as follows: "-jyeṣṭhaḥ a." —> "-jyeṣṭha a." LB AND AGAIN, we have a Global Correction as follows: "aḥ a." —> "a a." , whereby the abbreviation "a." in italics, indicates "adjective". Of course, there maybe some "Crossfire" in the event of a Masculine Noun, which would then require the change as: "aḥ a." —> "aḥ a" ; Although this Global Correction may require some "Manual Examination", it will save us both time to Correct All Such Instances In A Single Pass, rather than individually, one by one, over an extended period of time.
